message:
fix(node): replace unsafe 'as u32' casts with u32::try_from().unwrap_or(u32::MAX) in 6 modules

Direct 'len() as u32' and 'count() as u32' casts silently truncate on
collections exceeding u32::MAX (4 billion) items. While unlikely in normal
operation, saturating at u32::MAX is strictly safer than wrapping to zero,
which could cause logic errors in quorum counting, rate limiting, and
benchmark aggregation.

Modules updated:
- api/fleet_control_routes.rs: target_nodes count for command coordination
- api/verifier_routes.rs: conformance check counts (passed, failed, total)
- connector/durability.rs: quorum acknowledgement response counts
- policy/hardening_clamps.rs: escalation history event count
- runtime/crash_loop_detector.rs: sliding window crash count
- tools/benchmark_suite.rs: raw measurement and result list lengths

All use the pattern: u32::try_from(value).unwrap_or(u32::MAX)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -256,8 +256,8 @@ pub fn execute_coordination(
         command_id,
         command_type: request.command_type.clone(),
         participating_nodes: request.target_nodes.clone(),
-        ack_count: request.target_nodes.len() as u32,
-        total_nodes: request.target_nodes.len() as u32,
+        ack_count: u32::try_from(request.target_nodes.len()).unwrap_or(u32::MAX),
+        total_nodes: u32::try_from(request.target_nodes.len()).unwrap_or(u32::MAX),
         status: CoordinationStatus::Acknowledged,
         issued_at: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
     };

@@ -159,14 +159,20 @@ pub fn trigger_conformance(
         },
     ];
 
-    let passed = findings
-        .iter()
-        .filter(|f| f.status == ConformanceStatus::Pass)
-        .count() as u32;
-    let failed = findings
-        .iter()
-        .filter(|f| f.status == ConformanceStatus::Fail)
-        .count() as u32;
+    let passed = u32::try_from(
+        findings
+            .iter()
+            .filter(|f| f.status == ConformanceStatus::Pass)
+            .count(),
+    )
+    .unwrap_or(u32::MAX);
+    let failed = u32::try_from(
+        findings
+            .iter()
+            .filter(|f| f.status == ConformanceStatus::Fail)
+            .count(),
+    )
+    .unwrap_or(u32::MAX);
 
     let status = if failed > 0 {
         ConformanceStatus::Fail
@@ -177,7 +183,7 @@ pub fn trigger_conformance(
     let result = ConformanceResult {
         check_id,
         status,
-        total_checks: findings.len() as u32,
+        total_checks: u32::try_from(findings.len()).unwrap_or(u32::MAX),
         passed,
         failed,
         skipped: 0,
